mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index signals expanding manufacturing and utility output that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of rising-INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the mean return in triggered episodes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate and a decrease in industrial production amplifies recession probabilities beyond their independent effects, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling INDPRO fails to underperform the independent UNRATE-only and INDPRO-only signals by at least a factor of two in downside magnitude, or produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of joint-signal months.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month deceleration in consumer price inflation reduces expected terminal policy rates and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During episodes of FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL decelerations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the signal's information coefficient is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS declines are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of historical instances, or the mean forward return in triggered months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates labor market expansion that supports aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: In an elevated inflation regime, month-over-month increases in industrial production demonstrate supply-side resilience that reduces stagflation risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During months with CPIAUCSL > 190, month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals a contracting labor market that triggers recession expectations and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over any 2-year rolling window.
